currentSliceTitle: Plan additive neutral encounter validator and report support before production migration
currentSliceNotes: Neutral encounter representation planning is complete in docs/neutral-encounter-representation-plan.md. The target is a hybrid model with visible_stack as the default, camp_anchor as an explicit subtype, guard-linked variants, guard-target links, danger/readability cues, cleared-state policy, placement ownership, reward/guard summaries, passability/interaction implications, AI/editor placeholders, scenario placement compatibility, save/state implications, validation stages, migration sequence, rollback, and neutral_encounter_representation_bundle_001 as a tiny candidate planning bundle only. The next slice is report-only validator planning for current direct scenarios[].encounters[] compatibility inference. Production content JSON migration, renderer/pathing/AI/editor/runtime behavior changes, generated PNG import, route effects, body_tiles, and approach adoption remain out of scope.

realityAssessment.notes: Existing systems, JSON scaffolds, terrain/editor work, and validation harnesses remain useful foundations, but do not count as production depth or product completion until world/content/systems foundations and live-client manual play prove the loops.

phases.title: Honest reset, parity ledger, and removal of fake-complete language
phases.focus: Planning docs state the prototype/pre-alpha reality and reset the roadmap around manual play evidence.

phases.title: Make River Pass manually completable end-to-end
phases.focus: AcOrP reported River Pass manual completion gate passed on 2026-04-18, covering the live-client overworld, town, battle, save/resume, victory, defeat, and outcome-routing gate.

phases.title: Deep production foundation before broad maps and final polish
phases.focus: Build the missing world, faction, concept-art, economy, overworld object, magic, artifact, animation, and strategic AI foundations before treating campaign/skirmish maps or final screen polish as the center of work.

phases.title: Playable alpha baseline with deeply realized factions
phases.focus: After the foundation is deep enough, prove selected factions through scenario, town, battle, AI, save/load, economy, and manual-play slices before claiming playability.

phases.title: Production alpha layer for external playtest readiness
phases.focus: Coherent placeholder art/audio, settings, export/build pipeline, logs, onboarding, and external playtest workflow.

phases.title: HoMM2-class breadth
phases.focus: Playable broad content package with multiple original factions, maps, campaigns, AI, and reliable save/load.

phases.title: HoMM3-class breadth
phases.focus: Later strategic depth expansion after HoMM2-class breadth is stable and playable.

activeMilestone.goal: Stop treating current scaffolds as enough and build the deeper world/content/systems foundation required for a shippable fantasy strategy game.
targetSurfaces: World story and worldbuilding bible: premise, tone, geography, conflicts, history, and visual identity
Faction identity bible: unique original factions with world-fit backstory, economy, town, hero, unit, and strategic identities
Image-generation concept art pipeline for world mood, factions, towns, units, buildings, and heroes before implementation/deep polish
Economy overhaul plan covering multiple resources, faction preferences, mines/sites, pressure, costs, collection, and capture loops
Overworld object taxonomy and density plan covering decoration, interactable sites/buildings, and neutral unit encounters
Magic, artifact, animation, and strategic AI expansion plans
acceptanceCriteria: The project docs make clear that post-River-Pass polish is superseded by deep foundation work.
World story and faction identity work come before more campaign/skirmish map production.
Concept art using image generation is documented as a required design/art-direction stage before implementation or deep polish of major content.
Economy work is framed beyond gold, ore, and wood, including resources, mines/sites, pressure, costs, collection routes, and capture loops.
Overworld object work is framed around taxonomy and density rather than random flavor placement or generic markers.
Magic, artifacts, animation systems, and strategic AI are listed as foundation tracks before final maps/screens/game-loop polish.
Campaign/skirmish maps, town-screen polish, battle-screen polish, battle AI depth, and full-loop polish are explicitly sequenced after these foundations are deep enough.

steps.title: Realign project docs and progress tracker around the deep production foundation direction
Define richer world premise, tone, geography, conflicts, history, and visual identity
Redefine each faction as a unique world-grounded identity rather than a scaffold
Establish required image-generation concept art pipeline for world, factions, towns, units, buildings, and heroes
Plan the multi-resource economy, faction preferences, mines/sites, pressure, costs, collection, and capture loops
Plan overworld object taxonomy and density for decoration, interactable sites/buildings, and neutral encounters
Plan expanded magic schools, categories, faction/unit interactions, economy/artifact hooks, and adventure/tactical roles
Plan artifact breadth, unique sets, spell interactions, faction-specific interactions, and build implications
Plan animation systems for units, heroes, towns, map objects, UI feedback, and battle readability
Plan strategic AI for computer hero/town turns, economy, recruiting/building, movement, and objectives
Generate first actual concept-art studies for world mood and faction direction, then deliver images externally
Curate first external concept-art batch and prepare second-pass concept-art or implementation-brief inputs
Generate and curate remaining second-pass concept-art studies for faction, town, unit, and object readiness
Generate and curate broader overworld object and town studies before implementation briefs
Prepare implementation briefs for selected accepted concept-art directions
Plan overworld object schema migration from concept-art briefs
Plan economy/resource schema migration as the companion to object schema work
Plan first additive resource schema fields and validator warning reports
Implement additive resource validator/report scaffolding
Implement additive overworld object validator/report scaffolding
Review additive overworld object report output before any production schema migration
Plan first production additive overworld object schema fields for safe metadata only
Implement first production additive overworld object schema fields for the safe metadata bundle
Plan first-class visible neutral encounter representation before production migration
Plan additive neutral encounter validator and report support before production migration
After foundation depth, return to campaign/skirmish maps, town/battle screen polish, battle AI, and full-loop polish
Superseded post-River-Pass alpha content and presentation polish planning
Reconcile project docs and progress tracker after the post-River-Pass pivot
Repair renderer regression after overworld map render cache split
Reset project and plan docs to the prototype/pre-alpha reality
Draft a six-faction content bible with no commons or shared faction skeletons
Start real six-faction implementation with JSON content scaffolds and validation
Define overworld biomes, objects, buildings, and resource grammar
Implement biome and richer overworld object content domains
Implement first real neutral dwellings and neutral units for overworld and battle
Expand neutral dwellings to 25 authored content families
Author one 64x64 scenario that places the broad faction, biome, dwelling, and site-family scaffold
Fix overworld default framing so large maps open at a tactical zoom instead of fitting the whole map
Fix remembered-object readability, large-map panning, and remote owned-town entry
Improve overworld object marker readability on visible and remembered tiles
Keep explored overworld terrain fully visible outside the current scout net
Allow diagonal overworld movement at the same one-point cost as cardinal movement
Prepare and wire newly cut overworld terrain and object assets
Replace sampled terrain textures with an authored terrain grammar foundation
Use first authored overworld terrain tile art in the renderer
Replace terrain tile art with generated source terrain cuts
Correct overworld terrain away from generated-source tiling
Ground overworld markers into terrain instead of UI plates
Push overworld markers toward object-first placed presence
Deepen overworld terrain-contact and depth cues
Add footprint-aware overworld placement beds
Add upper-mass overworld backdrop cues
Integrate first town and encounter overworld placeholder assets
Present towns as 3x2 overworld footprints with a bottom-middle approach tile
Quiet town overworld grounding without base ellipses
Remove editor-like helper cues from town overworld presentation
Present the active hero as a placed overworld presence instead of a staged badge
Correct base terrain and structural road tile readability
Correct grass/plains consistency, terrain transitions, and road intersections after AcOrP feedback
Suppress black seams between visible explored overworld terrain tiles
Ground procedural fallback resource, artifact, and encounter objects without shared marker plates
Ground mapped resource, artifact, and encounter sprites without shared support stacks
Correct diagonal overworld road tile pieces and joins
Move overworld road rendering toward HoMM-style adjacency-built pieces
Start a thin in-game map editor shell for live scenario iteration
Add in-memory overworld object placement editing to the map editor
Preserve and resume the map editor working copy across Play Copy
Edit selected overworld object runtime properties in the map editor working copy
Move existing overworld object placements in the map editor working copy
Duplicate existing overworld object placements in the map editor working copy
Retheme existing overworld object placements in the map editor working copy
Flood-fill selected terrain regions in the map editor working copy
Paint compact road paths in the map editor working copy
Paint compact terrain lines in the map editor working copy
Paint compact terrain rectangles in the map editor working copy
Run a narrow Rubberduck terrain-surface feel test through the existing tile bank
Correct Rubberduck grassland base-tile cohesion
Prototype HoMM3-style neighbor-aware terrain and road rendering locally
Select overworld terrain transitions from neighboring terrain relationships
Narrow map editor base terrain options to HoMM3-style terrain families
Plan the HoMM3 terrain renderer rewrite from reconstruction evidence
Harden HoMM3 terrain renderer data-contract groundwork
Implement HoMM3 full land receiver stamp lookup
Make HoMM3 editor terrain restamp behavior explicit
Port HoMM3 terrain ownership queue placement into the map editor
Fix sand-heavy corner quadrant ownership regression
Replace Godot HoMM3 terrain visual selection with accepted web relation-class parity
Preserve accepted direct water/rock fallback truth signal
Expose accepted web prototype quadrant reprojection through Godot validation
Correct HoMM3 selected frame block payload truth
Restore accepted HoMM3 mixed-corner class reason payloads
Reduce the map editor preview zoom-out override to a practical span
Run a live-client manual audit of River Pass and record the first real player blockers
Create the River Pass parity ledger for overworld, town, battle, save/resume, victory, and defeat
Fix launch, objective, opening guidance, and overworld readability blockers
Recover owned-town usability for recruitment, recovery, garrison, and return routing
Recover battle usability for turn clarity, legal actions, targeting, enemy turns, and battle resolution

Complete River Pass manually at least twice from a clean profile and capture remaining friction

nextActions: Use docs/concept-art-pipeline.md as a required art-direction gate before economy-related resource-site, mine, artifact, faction town, or map-object visuals move into implementation.
Use docs/overworld-object-taxonomy-density.md as the object taxonomy and density source before object JSON migration, broad object placement, map-editor object expansion, or final object art.
Use docs/magic-system-expansion-foundation.md as the magic source before spell schema migration, magic UI, AI casting, adventure-map spell work, or spell/artifact integration.

Use docs/artifact-system-expansion-foundation.md as the artifact source before artifact JSON migration, set implementation, artifact reward tables, artifact UI, AI valuation, or spell/economy hook work.

Use docs/animation-systems-foundation.md as the animation source before unit animation, hero motion, town ambience, object state animation, spell/artifact VFX, UI motion, cue catalog, audio cue, or final battle/town polish work.

Use docs/strategic-ai-foundation.md as the strategic AI source before AI profile/schema migration, planner/executor work, AI event surfacing, town governors, real AI heroes, object valuation, spell/artifact planning, difficulty tuning, or broad map reliance on AI.

Use docs/concept-art-batch-001-review.md, docs/concept-art-batch-002-review.md, docs/concept-art-batch-003-review.md, docs/concept-art-batch-004-review.md, and docs/concept-art-batch-005-review.md to collect AcOrP accept/reject/defer calls on the external world/faction/object batch and second-pass follow-ups.

Use docs/concept-art-implementation-briefs.md as the first implementation-brief source for Embercourt town/object direction, Mireclaw town/object direction, and core overworld object classes while AcOrP curation remains open.

Use docs/overworld-object-schema-migration-plan.md, docs/overworld-object-safe-additive-schema-plan.md, and docs/neutral-encounter-representation-plan.md as the planning-only contracts before object JSON migration, map placement, renderer sprite ingestion, true footprint/pathing adoption, first-class neutral encounter metadata migration, or runtime asset import.

Plan additive neutral encounter validator/report support next: infer compatibility summaries from current direct scenarios[].encounters[] placements, define warning policy and strict non-production fixtures, and keep production JSON/runtime behavior unchanged.

Only after foundation depth and reviewed art-direction evidence start to exist, resume focus on implementation planning, campaign/skirmish maps, town-screen polish, battle-screen polish, battle AI systems, and the overall game loop.
Keep River Pass as completed proof history and Ninefold Confluence as breadth/loading evidence, not as current production-depth proof.
Avoid claiming six-faction, HoMM2-class, or HoMM3-class readiness until world, content, systems, AI, save/load, and manual-play evidence support it.
